editableNewDialogue.text: I’m trying to write a short response for a class discussion board. Can you help me keep it clear and not too long?
Yes. Paste what you have (or the prompt), and tell me the target length (like 5–7 sentences) and whether it should sound formal, casual, or in-between.
It’s about why people disagreed with a war, but I keep drifting into extra details. Any quick structure I can follow?

Use a tight 5-sentence structure: (1) main claim, (2) reason #1 with one concrete example, (3) reason #2 with one example, (4) a sentence on how effective the opposition was, (5) a closing sentence that ties back to the prompt. If you must cite, include one short quote and a parenthetical source at the end of the sentence it supports.
Switching topics: is there a simple way to explain why WebSockets aren’t the same as a custom TCP game protocol?

WebSockets start as HTTP(S) and then “upgrade” to a framed message protocol, with browser-style expectations (origins, proxies, etc.). A custom TCP game protocol is raw TCP with its own handshake, packet framing, compression, encryption, and version rules. Because the handshakes and framing are different, a WebSocket server can’t understand a game client unless something in the middle translates between them.
Got it. Also, totally random: I saw a note that some parks stopped dolphin shows in the 2010s—does that usually change how they’re regulated?

Often, yes. When shows are reduced or ended, facilities may shift to “education” or “presentation” formats, but regulations usually still focus on welfare standards: enclosure requirements, enrichment, veterinary care, transport rules, and reporting. The exact changes depend on the country and which agency licenses the facility.
Before I test the proxy code, I’m going to run it on my old laptop since it can sit next to the router and stay plugged in 24/7—my main PC keeps going to sleep.

That’s a good setup for a proxy. Put Node on the laptop, bind the proxy to 0.0.0.0 (not just localhost), and then point your Minecraft client at the laptop’s LAN IP. If you want it reliable, also disable sleep on that laptop and consider a static DHCP lease so the IP doesn’t change.
For movies, I actually don’t care about the storyline that much. I’m mostly here for action and cool set pieces—stuff like John Wick, Mad Max: Fury Road, and big fight scenes.
Got it—so you’re not really looking for heavy plot, you want the action to be the main thing.
Yeah. Interstellar and Inception were fine, but they’re not why I watch sci‑fi. I’d pick fast-paced action over a deep story any day.
Makes sense. I’ll focus on action-forward sci‑fi, not story-first picks.
